criteria.reasoning: yes — switching Library → Workbench without opening an entry retained prompt text and bindings. Evidence: full_run_flow_with_bindings_and_variants-shared.png

criteria.name: last_library_delete_shows_empty_state
criteria.description: Edge: after deleting every library entry, the library empty state is clear and visible with a control that returns to the workbench.
criteria.reasoning: yes — after deleting all entries, the app showed “Your library is empty” and Return to Workbench. Evidence: last_library_delete_shows_empty_state-shared.png

criteria.name: suggestion_chip_replaces_editor_content
criteria.description: Suggestion-chip flow: clicking a suggestion chip replaces the editor content with exactly that chip's prompt text and moves focus to the editor.
criteria.reasoning: yes — Launch brief replaced the editor with its exact prompt and focus remained in the editor. Evidence: suggestion_chip_replaces_editor_content-shared.png

criteria.name: stop_mid_stream_freezes_response
criteria.description: Stop flow: start a run, activate Stop mid-stream, and confirm the response text stays frozen, no further text appends, the run's status shows stopped, and Run is enabled again.
criteria.reasoning: yes — Stop produced status Stopped, froze the partial response, and re-enabled Run. Evidence: stop_mid_stream_freezes_response-shared.png

criteria.name: save_modal_and_library_round_trip
criteria.description: Save overlay: Save opens a modal with title (1 to 80 characters) and technique (closed enum) fields matching the LibraryPrompt field contract; confirming adds the prompt to the library with a success toast and a row showing that title and technique; switching to Library shows the count increased by one; opening the new entry restores the same prompt text, bindings, and attachments in the workbench.
criteria.reasoning: yes — Save modal accepted title and enum technique, added one row with success toast, and reopening it restored prompt text and bindings. Evidence: delete_library_entry_updates_count-shared.png

criteria.name: reload_returns_seeded_baseline
criteria.description: Recovery/reload: a page reload returns the app to its seeded state — 6 models, 4 library prompts, 4 suggestion chips, an empty editor, and no run history — with no dead end requiring a second reload.
criteria.reasoning: yes — reload returned empty editor, 6 model options, 4 suggestion chips, zero variables/history, and Library showed 4 prompts. Evidence: reload_returns_seeded_baseline-shared.png

criteria.name: export_package_flow_with_field_contract
criteria.description: Export package flow: edit the prompt and bind a variable, open Export, confirm Markdown and JSON previews contain the edited prompt text and binding value and the JSON shows schemaVersion prompt-package-v1 plus model, promptText, messages, bindings, and attachments, then Import that JSON and confirm the workbench restores the same text and bindings.
criteria.reasoning: yes — Markdown and JSON previews contained edited text/bindings; JSON showed prompt-package-v1 and required fields; importing restored the workbench. Evidence: full_run_flow_with_bindings_and_variants-shared.png

criteria.name: two_thirds_one_third_layout
criteria.description: At 1440px width the editor and response panel stack occupies roughly the left two-thirds of the viewport and the variable binding panel occupies the right one-third, as distinct visual regions
criteria.reasoning: The 1440px view clearly shows the editor/response stack occupying about two-thirds and the variable bindings panel occupying the right third. Evidence: initial-1440.png

criteria.name: monospace_editor_placeholder_highlight
criteria.description: The editor renders in a monospace typeface, double-brace placeholders carry a blue tag-style background highlight, and unbound placeholders in the preview carry an amber warning highlight clearly distinct from the bound-value rendering
criteria.reasoning: The editor uses a monospace font with blue-highlighted double-brace placeholders; the preview shows unbound placeholders in amber and bound values in green. Evidence: monospace-placeholders.png, bound-preview.png

criteria.name: toolbar_strip_complete
criteria.description: A single toolbar strip across the top of the editor contains the model selector, the token count badge, and the Insert Variable, Run, and Save controls, sharing one consistent visual treatment
criteria.reasoning: A single styled toolbar contains the model selector, token and estimate badges, Insert Variable, Run, and Save controls. Evidence: initial-1440.png

criteria.name: response_panel_code_container
criteria.description: The response panel renders as a code-snippet-style container with a visible streaming cursor indicator while a response is in progress
criteria.reasoning: Running the prompt produced a dark code-style response container with a visible streaming state and live draft content. Evidence: response-streaming.png

criteria.name: status_color_language
criteria.description: Run status uses a consistent color language across the status affordance and history rows: neutral for waiting, an active accent while streaming, a success treatment when complete, and a distinct treatment for stopped
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Neutral, active streaming, and successful completion states were observed with distinct color treatments, but the stopped state could not be successfully activated and verified. Evidence: initial-1440.png, response-streaming.png, response-complete.png

criteria.name: component_states_and_icons
criteria.description: Buttons, inputs, selects, and chips show distinct default, hover, focus (visible ring), and disabled treatments, and one consistent icon set is used across the toolbar, badges, chips, and history rows
criteria.reasoning: The UI visibly uses a consistent icon treatment, styled default/disabled controls, a hover wash, and a blue focus ring on inputs. Evidence: component-hover.png

criteria.name: typographic_hierarchy
criteria.description: Panel titles are visibly larger than metadata rows and helper text, and the hierarchy is consistent across the workbench and library views with a regular spacing rhythm between panels
criteria.reasoning: Panel headings are visibly larger than metadata/helper text, with consistent spacing across the workbench and library views. Evidence: initial-1440.png, library-1440.png

criteria.name: responsive_single_column
criteria.description: Below 1024px width the variable binding panel moves below the editor stack into a single column with every control still reachable, and at 375px width no content clips, nothing overflows, and no page-level horizontal scrollbar appears while the toolbar wraps its controls
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: At 900px the binding panel moves below the editor/response stack, but at 375px the suggestion-chip row visibly clips horizontally even though page scroll width remains within the viewport. Evidence: responsive-900.png, responsive-375.png

criteria.name: default_browser_styling_visible
criteria.description: Prominent controls (toolbar buttons, selects, modal fields, chips) render with unstyled browser-default appearance, or text and badges overlap or clip their containers at 1440px width
criteria.reasoning: The controls, selects, chips, modals/fields, and badges are visibly custom-styled without overlap or clipping at 1440px. Evidence: initial-1440.png

criteria.name: visual_design_catchall
criteria.description: The app exhibits a significant, browser-observable defect in visual design that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. 'Significant' means it would plausibly matter to a real user, not a nitpick. If present, name the defect and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the defect is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: No additional significant visual defect outside the covered criteria was observed. Evidence: initial-1440.png

criteria.name: consistent_capitalization_workbench_labels
criteria.description: Where the app renders panel titles, toolbar labels (Insert Variable, Run, Stop, Save), and library headings, they use one consistent capitalization convention throughout.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Multi-word headings use sentence case (Prompt editor, Variable bindings, Prompt library) while toolbar labels include title case (Insert Variable). Evidence: initial-workbench.png

criteria.name: specific_verbs_on_actions
criteria.description: Where the app renders button or action labels, they use specific labels such as Insert Variable, Run, Stop, and Save rather than generic Submit/OK when a specific label is possible.
criteria.reasoning: Specific action labels such as Insert Variable, Run, Save, Export, and Create prompt are rendered; no generic Submit/OK labels were observed. Evidence: initial-workbench.png

criteria.name: validation_names_problem_and_fix
criteria.description: Where the app renders Insert Variable, Save, or Import validation messages, they name the field and the field-contract fix (for example allowed letters/digits/underscores, title length, technique enum, or schemaVersion prompt-package-v1), not only a bare rejection like 'Invalid'.
criteria.reasoning: Validation messages name both the issue and fix: variable names must use letters, digits, and underscores, and schemaVersion must be exactly prompt-package-v1. Evidence: validation-variable.png, validation-import.png

criteria.name: empty_library_explains_next_step
criteria.description: Where the app renders the library empty state, the copy explains that no saved prompts remain and how to return to the workbench.
criteria.reasoning: No empty library state was rendered; the library showed saved prompts, so this conditional criterion is not applicable. Evidence: library-empty.png

criteria.name: workbench_copy_polished
criteria.description: Where the app renders toolbar, binding-panel, preview warning, and library copy, rate how free the text is of spelling and grammatical errors.
criteria.reasoning: Rendered toolbar, bindings, preview, and library copy was clear, grammatical, and free of observed spelling errors. Evidence: prompt-bound.png, library-empty.png

criteria.name: terminology_consistent_across_surfaces
criteria.description: Where the app renders labels for the same concept in multiple places (prompt vs library prompt, variable vs placeholder, run vs response), terminology stays consistent rather than inventing conflicting names.
criteria.reasoning: Prompt, variable, placeholder, bindings, preview, and response terminology remained consistent across the observed surfaces. Evidence: prompt-bound.png

criteria.name: token_and_timestamp_formatting_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders token counts, run timestamps, and variant position labels (for example 2 of 3), formatting is consistent across toolbar, history rows, and variant controls.
criteria.reasoning: The rendered token count uses a clear integer plus “tokens” format; no conflicting timestamp or variant-position surfaces were rendered. Evidence: prompt-bound.png, library-empty.png

criteria.name: success_messages_are_specific
criteria.description: Where the app renders confirmation feedback (Save success toast, code-block copy confirmation), the copy states what happened rather than a vague affirmation alone.
criteria.reasoning: The save confirmation explicitly states “Quarterly summary” saved to library rather than using a vague affirmation. Evidence: save-success.png

criteria.name: placeholder_copy_visible
criteria.description: Lorem ipsum or placeholder text ('TODO', 'Lorem', 'asdf', or template variable names such as '{{title}}') is visible anywhere in the shipped UI.
criteria.reasoning: Template variables such as {{report}}, {{product}}, and {{audience}} are visibly rendered in the workbench and library. Evidence: prompt-bound.png, library-empty.png

criteria.name: prominent_copy_has_spelling_errors
criteria.description: Spelling or grammatical errors appear in prominent copy such as Insert Variable, Run, Stop, Save, Unbound, or library empty-state text.
criteria.reasoning: No spelling or grammatical errors were observed in prominent labels or empty-state copy. Evidence: initial-workbench.png, validation-variable.png

criteria.name: inconsistent_concept_naming
criteria.description: The same prompt, variable, or run concept is named inconsistently across editor, bindings, preview, history, and library in a way that would confuse a user.
criteria.reasoning: The same prompt, variable, preview, and response concepts use consistent names across editor, bindings, preview, and library. Evidence: prompt-bound.png, library-empty.png

criteria.name: truncated_or_overflowing_labels
criteria.description: Toolbar labels, validation messages, or Unbound indicators are truncated or overflowing so the copy is unreadable.
criteria.reasoning: Labels and validation messages were readable; wrapped validation copy remained legible and no persistent overflow was observed. Evidence: validation-variable.png, validation-import.png

'Significant' means it would plausibly matter to a real user, not a nitpick. If present, name the defect and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the defect is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: No additional significant copy-quality defect beyond the capitalization issue already covered was observed. Evidence: initial-workbench.png, library-empty.png
